testcase.name: test_max_batch_size

failure.message: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'segment.analytics.request._session'; 'segment.analytics.request' is not a package

testcase.failure:
self = <analytics.test.test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_max_batch_size>
def test_max_batch_size(self): q = Queue() consumer = Consumer( q, 'testsecret', upload_size=100000, upload_interval=3) track = { 'type': 'track', 'event': 'python event', 'userId': 'userId' } msg_size = len(json.dumps(track).encode()) # number of messages in a maximum-size batch n_msgs = int(475000 / msg_size)
def mock_post_fn(_, data, **kwargs): res = mock.Mock() res.status_code = 200 self.assertTrue(len(data.encode()) < 500000, 'batch size (%d) exceeds 500KB limit' % len(data.encode())) return res
> with mock.patch('segment.analytics.request._session.post', side_effect=mock_post_fn) as mock_post:
segment/analytics/test/test_consumer.py:193: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/mock/mock.py:1353: in __enter__ self.target = self.getter() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/mock/mock.py:1523: in <lambda> getter = lambda: _importer(target) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/mock/mock.py:1210: in _importer thing = _dot_lookup(thing, comp, import_path) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
thing = <module 'segment.analytics.request' from '/Users/nelsonmbigili/Desktop/Spring 2026/Software Analysis/migratelib-artifact/repos/segmentio@analytics-python__603632f3__requests__httpx/segment/analytics/request.py'> comp = '_session', import_path = 'segment.analytics.request._session'
def _dot_lookup(thing, comp, import_path): try: return getattr(thing, comp) except AttributeError: > __import__(import_path) E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'segment.analytics.request._session'; 'segment.analytics.request' is not a package
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/mock/mock.py:1199: ModuleNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_proxies

failure.message: TypeError: Client.post() got an unexpected keyword argument 'proxies'

testcase.name: test_oauth_fail_unrecoverably
test_oauth_fail_with_retries
test_oauth_rate_limit_delay

failure.message: AssertionError: Expected call: sleep(234) Not called

testcase.failure:
self = <analytics.test.test_oauth.TestOauthManager testMethod=test_oauth_rate_limit_delay> mock_sleep = <MagicMock name='sleep' spec='builtin_function_or_method' id='4356641936'> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4356813520'>
@mock.patch.object(httpx, 'post', side_effect=mocked_requests_get) @mock.patch('time.sleep', spec=time.sleep) # 429 uses sleep so it won't be interrupted def test_oauth_rate_limit_delay(self, mock_sleep, mock_post): manager = segment.analytics.oauth_manager.OauthManager("id", privatekey, "keyid", "http://127.0.0.1:429") manager._poller_loop() > mock_sleep.assert_called_with(234)
segment/analytics/test/test_oauth.py:109: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
_mock_self = <MagicMock name='sleep' spec='builtin_function_or_method' id='4356641936'> args = (234,), kwargs = {}, expected = 'sleep(234)'
def assert_called_with(_mock_self, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the mock was called with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" self = _mock_self if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) > raise AssertionError('Expected call: %s\nNot called' % (expected,)) E AssertionError: Expected call: sleep(234) E Not called
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/mock/mock.py:925: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_oauth_success
testcase.failure:
self = <analytics.test.test_oauth.TestOauthManager testMethod=test_oauth_success> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4356595920'>
@mock.patch.object(httpx, 'post', side_effect=mocked_requests_get) def test_oauth_success(self, mock_post): manager = segment.analytics.oauth_manager.OauthManager("id", privatekey, "keyid", "http://127.0.0.1:80") > self.assertEqual(manager.get_token(), "test_token") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
segment/analytics/test/test_oauth.py:79: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <segment.analytics.oauth_manager.OauthManager object at 0x103ac73d0>
def get_token(self): with self.token_mutex: if self.token: return self.token # No good token, start the loop self.log.debug("OAuth is enabled. No cached access token.") # Make sure we're not waiting an excessively long time (this will not cancel 429 waits) if self.thread and self.thread.is_alive(): self.thread.cancel() self.thread = threading.Timer(0,self._poller_loop) self.thread.daemon = True self.thread.start()
while True: # Wait for a token or error with self.token_mutex: if self.token: return self.token if self.error: error = self.error self.error = None > raise error E segment.analytics.consumer.FatalError: [Segment] [Errno 61] Connection refused)
segment/analytics/oauth_manager.py:61: FatalError
